ayahs.textArabic: ٱقۡتَرَبَتِ ٱلسَّاعَةُ وَٱنشَقَّ ٱلۡقَمَرُ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Iqtarabati alssaAAatu wainshaqqa alqamaru
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: The Hour has come near, and the moon has split [in two].
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: The Hour has drawn near and the moon was split ˹in two˺.

ayahs.textArabic: وَإِن يَرَوۡاْ ءَايَةٗ يُعۡرِضُواْ وَيَقُولُواْ سِحۡرٞ مُّسۡتَمِرّٞ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wain yaraw ayatan yuAAridoo wayaqooloo sihrun mustamirrun
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And if they see a miracle, they turn away and say, "Passing magic."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Yet, whenever they see a sign, they turn away, saying, “Same old magic!”

ayahs.textArabic: وَكَذَّبُواْ وَٱتَّبَعُوٓاْ أَهۡوَآءَهُمۡۚ وَكُلُّ أَمۡرٖ مُّسۡتَقِرّٞ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wakaththaboo waittabaAAoo ahwaahum wakullu amrin mustaqirrun
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And they denied and followed their inclinations. But for every matter is a [time of] settlement.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: They rejected ˹the truth˺ and followed their own desires—and every matter will be settled—

ayahs.textArabic: وَلَقَدۡ جَآءَهُم مِّنَ ٱلۡأَنۢبَآءِ مَا فِيهِ مُزۡدَجَرٌ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Walaqad jaahum mina alanbai ma feehi muzdajarun
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And there has already come to them of information that in which there is deterrence -
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: even though the stories ˹of destroyed nations˺ that have already come to them are a sufficient deterrent.

ayahs.textArabic: حِكۡمَةُۢ بَٰلِغَةٞۖ فَمَا تُغۡنِ ٱلنُّذُرُ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Hikmatun balighatun fama tughnee alnnuthuru
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Extensive wisdom - but warning does not avail [them].
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹This Quran is˺ profound ˹in˺ wisdom, but warnings are of no benefit ˹to them˺.

ayahs.textArabic: فَتَوَلَّ عَنۡهُمۡۘ يَوۡمَ يَدۡعُ ٱلدَّاعِ إِلَىٰ شَيۡءٖ نُّكُرٍ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Fatawalla AAanhum yawma yadAAu alddaAAi ila shayin nukurin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So leave them, [O Muhammad]. The Day the Caller calls to something forbidding,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So turn away from them ˹O Prophet˺. ˹And wait for˺ the Day ˹when˺ the caller will summon ˹them˺ for something horrifying.

ayahs.textArabic: خُشَّعًا أَبۡصَٰرُهُمۡ يَخۡرُجُونَ مِنَ ٱلۡأَجۡدَاثِ كَأَنَّهُمۡ جَرَادٞ مُّنتَشِرٞ
ayahs.textTransliteration: KhushshaAAan absaruhum yakhrujoona mina alajdathi kaannahum jaradun muntashirun
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Their eyes humbled, they will emerge from the graves as if they were locusts spreading,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: With eyes downcast, they will come forth from the graves as if they were swarming locusts,

ayahs.textArabic: مُّهۡطِعِينَ إِلَى ٱلدَّاعِۖ يَقُولُ ٱلۡكَٰفِرُونَ هَٰذَا يَوۡمٌ عَسِرٞ
ayahs.textTransliteration: MuhtiAAeena ila alddaAAi yaqoolu alkafiroona hatha yawmun AAasirun
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Racing ahead toward the Caller. The disbelievers will say, "This is a difficult Day."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: rushing towards the caller. The disbelievers will cry, “This is a difficult Day!”

ayahs.textArabic: ۞ كَذَّبَتۡ قَبۡلَهُمۡ قَوۡمُ نُوحٖ فَكَذَّبُواْ عَبۡدَنَا وَقَالُواْ مَجۡنُونٞ وَٱزۡدُجِرَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Kaththabat qablahum qawmu noohin fakaththaboo AAabdana waqaloo majnoonun waizdujira
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: The people of Noah denied before them, and they denied Our servant and said, "A madman," and he was repelled.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Before them, the people of Noah denied ˹the truth˺ and rejected Our servant, calling ˹him˺ insane. And he was intimidated.

ayahs.textArabic: فَدَعَا رَبَّهُۥٓ أَنِّي مَغۡلُوبٞ فَٱنتَصِرۡ
ayahs.textTransliteration: FadaAAa rabbahu annee maghloobun faintasir
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So he invoked his Lord, "Indeed, I am overpowered, so help."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So he cried out to his Lord, “I am helpless, so help ˹me˺!”

ayahs.textArabic: فَفَتَحۡنَآ أَبۡوَٰبَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ بِمَآءٖ مُّنۡهَمِرٖ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Fafatahna abwaba alssamai bimain munhamirin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then We opened the gates of the heaven with rain pouring down
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So We opened the gates of the sky with pouring rain,

ayahs.textArabic: وَفَجَّرۡنَا ٱلۡأَرۡضَ عُيُونٗا فَٱلۡتَقَى ٱلۡمَآءُ عَلَىٰٓ أَمۡرٖ قَدۡ قُدِرَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wafajjarna alarda AAuyoonan failtaqa almao AAala amrin qad qudira
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And caused the earth to burst with springs, and the waters met for a matter already predestined.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and caused the earth to burst with springs, so the waters met for a fate already set.

ayahs.textArabic: وَحَمَلۡنَٰهُ عَلَىٰ ذَاتِ أَلۡوَٰحٖ وَدُسُرٖ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wahamalnahu AAala thati alwahin wadusurin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And We carried him on a [construction of] planks and nails,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: We carried him on that ˹Ark made˺ of planks and nails,

ayahs.textArabic: تَجۡرِي بِأَعۡيُنِنَا جَزَآءٗ لِّمَن كَانَ كُفِرَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Tajree biaAAyunina jazaan liman kana kufira
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Sailing under Our observation as reward for he who had been denied.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: sailing under Our ˹watchful˺ Eyes—a ˹fair˺ punishment on behalf of the one ˹they˺ denied.

ayahs.textArabic: وَلَقَد تَّرَكۡنَٰهَآ ءَايَةٗ فَهَلۡ مِن مُّدَّكِرٖ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Walaqad taraknaha ayatan fahal min muddakirin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And We left it as a sign, so is there any who will remember?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: We certainly left this as a sign. So is there anyone who will be mindful?

ayahs.textArabic: فَكَيۡفَ كَانَ عَذَابِي وَنُذُرِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Fakayfa kana AAathabee wanuthuri
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And how [severe] were My punishment and warning.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then how ˹dreadful˺ were My punishment and warnings!

ayahs.textArabic: وَلَقَدۡ يَسَّرۡنَا ٱلۡقُرۡءَانَ لِلذِّكۡرِ فَهَلۡ مِن مُّدَّكِرٖ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Walaqad yassarna alqurana lilththikri fahal min muddakirin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And We have certainly made the Qur'an easy for remembrance, so is there any who will remember?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And We have certainly made the Quran easy to remember. So is there anyone who will be mindful?

ayahs.textArabic: كَذَّبَتۡ عَادٞ فَكَيۡفَ كَانَ عَذَابِي وَنُذُرِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Kaththabat AAadun fakayfa kana AAathabee wanuthuri
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: 'Aad denied; and how [severe] were My punishment and warning.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ’Ȃd ˹also˺ rejected ˹the truth˺. Then how ˹dreadful˺ were My punishment and warnings!

ayahs.textArabic: إِنَّآ أَرۡسَلۡنَا عَلَيۡهِمۡ رِيحٗا صَرۡصَرٗا فِي يَوۡمِ نَحۡسٖ مُّسۡتَمِرّٖ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Inna arsalna AAalayhim reehan sarsaran fee yawmi nahsin mustamirrin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed, We sent upon them a screaming wind on a day of continuous misfortune,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Indeed, We sent against them a furious wind, on a day of unrelenting misery,

ayahs.textArabic: تَنزِعُ ٱلنَّاسَ كَأَنَّهُمۡ أَعۡجَازُ نَخۡلٖ مُّنقَعِرٖ
ayahs.textTransliteration: TanziAAu alnnasa kaannahum aAAjazu nakhlin munqaAAirin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Extracting the people as if they were trunks of palm trees uprooted.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: that snatched people up, leaving them like trunks of uprooted palm trees.

ayahs.textArabic: فَكَيۡفَ كَانَ عَذَابِي وَنُذُرِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Fakayfa kana AAathabee wanuthuri
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And how [severe] were My punishment and warning.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then how ˹dreadful˺ were My punishment and warnings!

ayahs.textArabic: وَلَقَدۡ يَسَّرۡنَا ٱلۡقُرۡءَانَ لِلذِّكۡرِ فَهَلۡ مِن مُّدَّكِرٖ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Walaqad yassarna alqurana lilththikri fahal min muddakirin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And We have certainly made the Qur'an easy for remembrance, so is there any who will remember?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And We have certainly made the Quran easy to remember. So is there anyone who will be mindful?

ayahs.textArabic: كَذَّبَتۡ ثَمُودُ بِٱلنُّذُرِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Kaththabat thamoodu bialnnuthuri
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Thamud denied the warning
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Thamûd rejected the warnings ˹as well˺,

ayahs.textArabic: فَقَالُوٓاْ أَبَشَرٗا مِّنَّا وَٰحِدٗا نَّتَّبِعُهُۥٓ إِنَّآ إِذٗا لَّفِي ضَلَٰلٖ وَسُعُرٍ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Faqaloo abasharan minna wahidan nattabiAAuhu inna ithan lafee dalalin wasuAAurin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And said, "Is it one human being among us that we should follow? Indeed, we would then be in error and madness.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: arguing, “How can we follow one ˹average˺ human being from among us? We would then truly be misguided and insane.

ayahs.textArabic: أَءُلۡقِيَ ٱلذِّكۡرُ عَلَيۡهِ مِنۢ بَيۡنِنَا بَلۡ هُوَ كَذَّابٌ أَشِرٞ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Aolqiya alththikru AAalayhi min baynina bal huwa kaththabun ashirun
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Has the message been sent down upon him from among us? Rather, he is an insolent liar."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Has the revelation been sent down ˹only˺ to him out of ˹all of˺ us? In fact, he is a boastful liar.”

ayahs.textArabic: سَيَعۡلَمُونَ غَدٗا مَّنِ ٱلۡكَذَّابُ ٱلۡأَشِرُ
ayahs.textTransliteration: SayaAAlamoona ghadan mani alkaththabu alashiru
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: They will know tomorrow who is the insolent liar.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹It was revealed to Ṣâliḥ,˺ “They will soon know who the boastful liar is.

ayahs.textArabic: إِنَّا مُرۡسِلُواْ ٱلنَّاقَةِ فِتۡنَةٗ لَّهُمۡ فَٱرۡتَقِبۡهُمۡ وَٱصۡطَبِرۡ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Inna mursiloo alnnaqati fitnatan lahum fairtaqibhum waistabir
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed, We are sending the she-camel as trial for them, so watch them and be patient.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: We are sending the she-camel as a test for them. So watch them ˹closely˺, and have patience.

ayahs.textArabic: وَنَبِّئۡهُمۡ أَنَّ ٱلۡمَآءَ قِسۡمَةُۢ بَيۡنَهُمۡۖ كُلُّ شِرۡبٖ مُّحۡتَضَرٞ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wanabbihum anna almaa qismatun baynahum kullu shirbin muhtadarun
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And inform them that the water is shared between them, each [day of] drink attended [by turn].
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And tell them that the ˹drinking˺ water must be divided between them ˹and her˺, each taking a turn to drink ˹every other day˺.”

ayahs.textArabic: فَنَادَوۡاْ صَاحِبَهُمۡ فَتَعَاطَىٰ فَعَقَرَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Fanadaw sahibahum fataAAata faAAaqara
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: But they called their companion, and he dared and hamstrung [her].
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: But they roused a companion of theirs, so he dared to kill ˹her˺.

ayahs.textArabic: فَكَيۡفَ كَانَ عَذَابِي وَنُذُرِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Fakayfa kana AAathabee wanuthuri
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And how [severe] were My punishment and warning.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then how ˹dreadful˺ were My punishment and warnings!

ayahs.textArabic: إِنَّآ أَرۡسَلۡنَا عَلَيۡهِمۡ صَيۡحَةٗ وَٰحِدَةٗ فَكَانُواْ كَهَشِيمِ ٱلۡمُحۡتَظِرِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Inna arsalna AAalayhim sayhatan wahidatan fakanoo kahasheemi almuhtathiri
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed, We sent upon them one blast from the sky, and they became like the dry twig fragments of an [animal] pen.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Indeed, We sent against them ˹only˺ one ˹mighty˺ blast, leaving them like the twigs of fence-builders.

ayahs.textArabic: وَلَقَدۡ يَسَّرۡنَا ٱلۡقُرۡءَانَ لِلذِّكۡرِ فَهَلۡ مِن مُّدَّكِرٖ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Walaqad yassarna alqurana lilththikri fahal min muddakirin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And We have certainly made the Qur'an easy for remembrance, so is there any who will remember?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And We have certainly made the Quran easy to remember. So is there anyone who will be mindful?

ayahs.textArabic: كَذَّبَتۡ قَوۡمُ لُوطِۭ بِٱلنُّذُرِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Kaththabat qawmu lootin bialnnuthuri
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: The people of Lot denied the warning.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: The people of Lot ˹also˺ rejected the warnings.

ayahs.textArabic: إِنَّآ أَرۡسَلۡنَا عَلَيۡهِمۡ حَاصِبًا إِلَّآ ءَالَ لُوطٖۖ نَّجَّيۡنَٰهُم بِسَحَرٖ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Inna arsalna AAalayhim hasiban illa ala lootin najjaynahum bisaharin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed, We sent upon them a storm of stones, except the family of Lot - We saved them before dawn
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: We unleashed upon them a storm of stones. As for ˹the believers of˺ Lot’s family, We delivered them before dawn

ayahs.textArabic: نِّعۡمَةٗ مِّنۡ عِندِنَاۚ كَذَٰلِكَ نَجۡزِي مَن شَكَرَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: NiAAmatan min AAindina kathalika najzee man shakara
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: As favor from us. Thus do We reward he who is grateful.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: as a blessing from Us. This is how We reward whoever gives thanks.

ayahs.textArabic: وَلَقَدۡ أَنذَرَهُم بَطۡشَتَنَا فَتَمَارَوۡاْ بِٱلنُّذُرِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Walaqad antharahum batshatana fatamaraw bialnnuthuri
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And he had already warned them of Our assault, but they disputed the warning.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: He had already warned them of Our ˹crushing˺ blow but they disputed the warnings.

ayahs.textArabic: وَلَقَدۡ رَٰوَدُوهُ عَن ضَيۡفِهِۦ فَطَمَسۡنَآ أَعۡيُنَهُمۡ فَذُوقُواْ عَذَابِي وَنُذُرِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Walaqad rawadoohu AAan dayfihi fatamasna aAAyunahum fathooqoo AAathabee wanuthuri
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And they had demanded from him his guests, but We obliterated their eyes, [saying], "Taste My punishment and warning."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And they even demanded his angel-guests from him, so We blinded their eyes. ˹And they were told,˺ “Taste then My punishment and warnings!”

ayahs.textArabic: وَلَقَدۡ صَبَّحَهُم بُكۡرَةً عَذَابٞ مُّسۡتَقِرّٞ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Walaqad sabbahahum bukratan AAathabun mustaqirrun
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And there came upon them by morning an abiding punishment.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And indeed, by the early morning they were overwhelmed by an unrelenting torment.

ayahs.textArabic: فَذُوقُواْ عَذَابِي وَنُذُرِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Fathooqoo AAathabee wanuthuri
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So taste My punishment and warning.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹Again they were told,˺ “Taste now My punishment and warnings!”

ayahs.textArabic: وَلَقَدۡ يَسَّرۡنَا ٱلۡقُرۡءَانَ لِلذِّكۡرِ فَهَلۡ مِن مُّدَّكِرٖ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Walaqad yassarna alqurana lilththikri fahal min muddakirin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And We have certainly made the Qur'an easy for remembrance, so is there any who will remember?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And We have certainly made the Quran easy to remember. So is there anyone who will be mindful?

ayahs.textArabic: وَلَقَدۡ جَآءَ ءَالَ فِرۡعَوۡنَ ٱلنُّذُرُ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Walaqad jaa ala firAAawna alnnuthuru
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And there certainly came to the people of Pharaoh warning.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And indeed, the warnings ˹also˺ came to the people of Pharaoh.

ayahs.textArabic: كَذَّبُواْ بِـَٔايَٰتِنَا كُلِّهَا فَأَخَذۡنَٰهُمۡ أَخۡذَ عَزِيزٖ مُّقۡتَدِرٍ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Kaththaboo biayatina kulliha faakhathnahum akhtha AAazeezin muqtadirin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: They denied Our signs, all of them, so We seized them with a seizure of one Exalted in Might and Perfect in Ability.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹But˺ they rejected all of Our signs, so We seized them with the ˹crushing˺ grip of the Almighty, Most Powerful.

ayahs.textArabic: أَكُفَّارُكُمۡ خَيۡرٞ مِّنۡ أُوْلَٰٓئِكُمۡ أَمۡ لَكُم بَرَآءَةٞ فِي ٱلزُّبُرِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Akuffarukum khayrun min olaikum am lakum baraatun fee alzzuburi
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Are your disbelievers better than those [former ones], or have you immunity in the scripture?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Now, are you ˹Meccan˺ disbelievers superior to those ˹destroyed peoples˺? Or have you ˹been granted˺ immunity ˹from punishment˺ in divine Books?

ayahs.textArabic: أَمۡ يَقُولُونَ نَحۡنُ جَمِيعٞ مُّنتَصِرٞ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Am yaqooloona nahnu jameeAAun muntasirun
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Or do they say, "We are an assembly supporting [each other]"?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Or do they say, “We are all ˹a˺ united ˹front˺, bound to prevail.”?

ayahs.textArabic: سَيُهۡزَمُ ٱلۡجَمۡعُ وَيُوَلُّونَ ٱلدُّبُرَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Sayuhzamu aljamAAu wayuwalloona alddubura
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [Their] assembly will be defeated, and they will turn their backs [in retreat].
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹Soon˺ their united front will be defeated and ˹forced to˺ flee.

ayahs.textArabic: بَلِ ٱلسَّاعَةُ مَوۡعِدُهُمۡ وَٱلسَّاعَةُ أَدۡهَىٰ وَأَمَرُّ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Bali alssaAAatu mawAAiduhum waalssaAAatu adha waamarru
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: But the Hour is their appointment [for due punishment], and the Hour is more disastrous and more bitter.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Better yet, the Hour is their appointed time—and the Hour will be most catastrophic and most bitter.

ayahs.textArabic: إِنَّ ٱلۡمُجۡرِمِينَ فِي ضَلَٰلٖ وَسُعُرٖ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Inna almujrimeena fee dalalin wasuAAurin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed, the criminals are in error and madness.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Indeed, the wicked are ˹entrenched˺ in misguidance, and ˹are bound for˺ blazes.

ayahs.textArabic: يَوۡمَ يُسۡحَبُونَ فِي ٱلنَّارِ عَلَىٰ وُجُوهِهِمۡ ذُوقُواْ مَسَّ سَقَرَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Yawma yushaboona fee alnnari AAala wujoohihim thooqoo massa saqara
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: The Day they are dragged into the Fire on their faces [it will be said], "Taste the touch of Saqar."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: On the Day they will be dragged into the Fire on their faces, ˹they will be told,˺ “Taste the touch of Hell!”

ayahs.textArabic: إِنَّا كُلَّ شَيۡءٍ خَلَقۡنَٰهُ بِقَدَرٖ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Inna kulla shayin khalaqnahu biqadarin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed, all things We created with predestination.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Indeed, We have created everything, perfectly preordained.

ayahs.textArabic: وَمَآ أَمۡرُنَآ إِلَّا وَٰحِدَةٞ كَلَمۡحِۭ بِٱلۡبَصَرِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wama amruna illa wahidatun kalamhin bialbasari
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And Our command is but one, like a glance of the eye.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Our command is but a single word, done in the blink of an eye.

ayahs.textArabic: وَلَقَدۡ أَهۡلَكۡنَآ أَشۡيَاعَكُمۡ فَهَلۡ مِن مُّدَّكِرٖ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Walaqad ahlakna ashyaAAakum fahal min muddakirin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And We have already destroyed your kinds, so is there any who will remember?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: We have already destroyed the likes of you. So will any ˹of you˺ be mindful?

ayahs.textArabic: وَكُلُّ شَيۡءٖ فَعَلُوهُ فِي ٱلزُّبُرِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wakullu shayin faAAaloohu fee alzzuburi
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And everything they did is in written records.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Everything they have done is ˹listed˺ in ˹their˺ records.

ayahs.textArabic: وَكُلُّ صَغِيرٖ وَكَبِيرٖ مُّسۡتَطَرٌ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wakullu sagheerin wakabeerin mustatarun
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And every small and great [thing] is inscribed.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Every matter, small and large, is written ˹precisely˺.

ayahs.textArabic: إِنَّ ٱلۡمُتَّقِينَ فِي جَنَّٰتٖ وَنَهَرٖ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Inna almuttaqeena fee jannatin wanaharin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed, the righteous will be among gardens and rivers,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Indeed, the righteous will be amid Gardens and rivers,

ayahs.textArabic: فِي مَقۡعَدِ صِدۡقٍ عِندَ مَلِيكٖ مُّقۡتَدِرِۭ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Fee maqAAadi sidqin AAinda maleekin muqtadirin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: In a seat of honor near a Sovereign, Perfect in Ability.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: at the Seat of Honour in the presence of the Most Powerful Sovereign.
